★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 1/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.42 4.12 3.20 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.91 0.72 0.85 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.27 0.92 0.25 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.24 2.49 2.10 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.18 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.01 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-02-03 12 12 0 64 1 64
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-01-11 16 14 3 132 1 132
Total weighted health score
81.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (99)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

72 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
20 G/H/I Actual harm
7 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0622 Not transfer or discharge a resident without an adequate reason; and must provide documentation and convey specific information when a resident is transferred or discharged. D 2025-02-03 2025-03-27
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2025-02-03 2025-03-27
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2025-02-03 2025-03-27
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2025-02-03 2025-03-27
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2025-02-03 2025-03-27
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2025-02-03 2025-03-27
0694 Provide for the safe, appropriate administration of IV fluids for a resident when needed. D 2025-02-03 2025-03-27
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2025-02-03 2025-03-27
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2025-02-03 2025-03-27
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. E 2025-02-03 2025-03-27
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2025-02-03 2025-03-27
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2025-02-03 2025-03-27
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. E 2024-01-11 2024-02-12
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2024-01-11 2024-02-12
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2024-01-11 2024-02-12
0779 Keep signed and dated reports of x-rays and other diagnostic services in the residents record. D 2024-01-11 2024-02-12
0801 Employ sufficient staff with the appropriate competencies and skills sets to carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service, including a qualified dietician. E 2024-01-11 2024-02-12
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. E 2024-01-11 2024-02-12
0805 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food prepared in a form designed to meet individual needs. D 2024-01-11 2024-02-12
0806 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food that accommodates resident allergies, intolerances, and preferences, as well as appealing options. D 2024-01-11 2024-02-12
0811 Ensure that residents are assessed for appropriateness for a feeding assistant program, receive services as per their plan of care, and feeding assistants are trained and supervised. D 2024-01-11 2024-02-12
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2024-01-11 2024-02-12
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. E 2024-01-11 2024-02-12
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2024-01-11 2024-02-12
0881 Implement a program that monitors antibiotic use. E 2024-01-11 2024-02-12
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. G 2024-01-11 2024-02-12
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-01-11 2024-02-12
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. G 2023-03-13 2023-04-04
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. E 2022-04-13 2022-06-23
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2022-04-13 2022-06-23
0561 Honor the resident's right to and the facility must promote and facilitate resident self-determination through support of resident choice. D 2022-04-13 2022-06-23
0565 Honor the resident's right to organize and participate in resident/family groups in the facility. E 2022-04-13 2022-06-23

0568 Properly hold, secure, and manage each resident's personal money which is deposited with the nursing home. E 2022-04-13 2022-06-23

0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. K 2022-04-13 2022-06-23

0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. K 2022-04-13 2022-06-23
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. K 2022-04-13 2022-06-23
